title: Laelia pumila
intro: Laelia pumila, also known as the dwarf orchid, is a terrestrial orchid species native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to promote health and well-being, with its leaves and roots offering potential benefits for treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Laelia pumila include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Laelia pumila is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infused tea to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Laelia pumila is a species of orchid native to Mexico. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. More specifically, it is classified within the subtribe Laeliinae. This classification places Laelia pumila within the genus Laelia. The exact classification within the genus is Laelia pumila (Lindl.) Schltr.
